Title: Govt can only advise, not force companies to adopt WFH, says HR minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan said the government cannot compel private companies to adopt work-from-home (WFH) policies as businesses are best placed to determine their productivity needs.
He said Putrajaya could only encourage and advise employers to adopt flexible working arrangements amid concerns over the global economic climate.
"Private companies, we cannot really dictate to them because only they know what their productivity is.
"We don't have the right to decide their productivity for them. That is beyond our guidelines and restrictions," he said after the phase one press conference for socio-economic development of the Malaysian Indian Transformation Unit (Mitra), here, today.
He was responding to questions on the low take-up rate of WFH policies among private sector companies despite repeated calls by the government.
Ramanan said the government would continue encouraging companies to adopt WFH arrangements following repeated reminders by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Of course, we encourage them as best as we can to adopt the work-from-home policy because the prime minister has repeatedly spoken about it and we have weekly briefings on the current global economic crisis.
"Due to this crisis, we will see the real impact in the months to come," he said.
He added that the government was putting "buffers" in place to help companies and workers manage any economic challenges ahead.
"So we are already putting all these buffers into place so that there will be ease and a soft landing for these companies.
"Otherwise, you're really going to hurt the industry and hurt your employees as well," he said.
Ramanan added that the government's role was limited to providing guidance and recommendations to employers.
"So the best we can do is advise. We cannot go and infringe upon them," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: "Please help mummy and daddy," woman pleads after court sets RM240,000 bail
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A woman broke down in tears inside the Sessions Court here today, pleading with her children to find help to raise the RM240,000 bail set for her and her husband after both were charged with trafficking Indonesian migrant workers for forced labour.
Chan Koon Pheng, 40, wept uncontrollably as she asked her children in Mandarin to beg relatives for financial assistance, crying that she did not want to go to prison.
"Please help mummy and daddy. Ask our relatives to help us.
"I don't want to stay inside the prison," she cried.
Chan and her husband, Tan Kok Teong, 42, jointly claimed trial to nine charges of trafficking Indonesian women for forced labour between August last year and May this year.
The offences were allegedly committed at several locations across the northeast, southwest, and Seberang Perai Tengah districts.
They were charged under Section 12 of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007, which carries a jail term of up to 20 years and a fine upon conviction.
Chan and Tan nodded their heads to indicate they understood the charges, which were read to them before Judge Ooi Sheow Yean.
They, however, pleaded not guilty.
Chan also personally claimed trial to four similar charges in the same court. Earlier, she had pleaded not guilty to two other similar charges before Judge Roslan Hamid.
Deputy public prosecutor Lee Jun Keong asked the court to set bail at RM20,000 for each accused for every charge.
He also requested that they surrender their passports, report to the nearest police station monthly, and refrain from contacting prosecution witnesses.
Defence counsel Arvend Rajan appealed for a lower bail, stating Chan was the sole breadwinner earning between RM3,000 and RM5,000 monthly to support their four children.
He added that the couple suffered from high blood pressure and diabetes, required daily medication, and had fully cooperated with the police since their arrest on May 12.
"My clients have been in custody since their arrests on May 12 and have rendered cooperation throughout police's investigations into the case.
"Both of them will also adhere to any additional conditions set by the court.
"An excessive bail amount is a punishment by itself. My clients are innocent unless proven otherwise. The bail is just to secure their attendance in court," he added.
Judges Ooi and Roslan both set bail at RM10,000 with one surety for each charge, bringing the total bail amount to RM240,000 for the couple.
Ooi fixed June 30 for mention pending document submissions, while Roslan set July 2.
As she was being led to the police van, Chan again cried out to her children for help. Arvend noted that his clients would need time to raise the required funds.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Najib seeks to adduce new evidence in RM2.27b 1MDB appeal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has moved to introduce fresh evidence in his appeal against the conviction and sentence imposed on him in the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) case involving RM2.27 billion.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission senior deputy director of the legal and prosecution division Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib confirmed the filing.
The new evidence centres on the involvement of businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, and former 1MDB general counsel Jasmine Loo Ai Swan.
Jho Low remains at large, while Loo previously testified as the prosecution's 50th witness during the 1MDB trial.
The application was filed at the Court of Appeal by Najib's legal team from Shafee & Co.
It is part of the former Pekan member of parliament's bid to overturn the High Court's decision in his second criminal trial linked to the multibillion-ringgit 1MDB scandal.
Najib's lead lawyer, Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, said he would only be available to comment after 6pm today as he was attending a conference.
Checks with the judiciary system showed that the application has been fixed for case management on June 19.
No appeal dates have been set so far, as parties are still waiting for the full written grounds of judgment from trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who has since been appointed to the Federal Court.
In December last year, Sequerah found Najib guilty on all 25 charges of abuse of power and money laundering connected to RM2.27 billion in 1MDB funds.
The 73-year-old was sentenced to 15 years' imprisonment and fined RM11.38 billion.
The court also ordered that Najib be jailed for an additional 10 years if he failed to pay the fine.
Najib was also directed to pay a recoverable sum of RM2.081 billion or serve an additional two years and six months in jail if it is not settled.
Najib is to serve the sentence after completing his current six-year term at Kajang Prison following his conviction for misappropriating RM42 million from SRC International Sdn Bhd.
He began serving the sentence on Aug 23, 2022.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Apex Equity to raise RM14.07mil via private placement for money-lending business
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Apex Equity Holdings Bhd has proposed to undertake a private placement of up to 10 per cent or up to 21.36 million new Apex shares to raise up to RM14.07 million.
The company said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia today that the amount raised is based on the indicative issue price of 65.9 sen per placement share.
The placement proceeds to be raised from the proposed private placement are expected to be utilised by Apex and its subsidiaries for the money lending business (RM13.52 million) and estimated expenses for the proposed private placement (RM558,000).
"The expansion of the money lending business is expected to generate a stable stream of interest income for the group, strengthen its market presence and competitiveness in the money lending business and contribute positively to its overall financial performance," it said.
It said the proposed private placement will serve as an additional source of funding for the group without incurring interest expenses as compared to conventional means of debt financing, in addition to strengthening the capital structure of the group by increasing the capital base of the group.
Also, it said the proposed private placement will provide an opportunity for the introduction of new investors to participate in the equity of the company, its future prospects and growth as well as broadening the shareholders base of the company.
As of May 15, 2026, being the latest practicable date preceding the date of the announcement, Apex has an issued share capital of RM221.94 million comprising 213.56 million shares.
On the group prospects, Apex said it remains confident on its strategic direction and long-term growth prospects despite lingering global uncertainties and evolving market conditions.
"The group will continue to diversify its income streams by strengthening contributions across its core businesses, broadening its product offerings, and investing in digital capabilities to enhance customer experience, operational efficiency, and scalability," it said.
Apex highlighted that it's money lending business, which has demonstrated encouraging momentum since its reactivation in the third quarter of 2022, is expected to remain an important complementary growth driver.
"Apex intends to strengthen its financing offerings and pursue disciplined customer acquisition while maintaining prudent underwriting standards, effective credit monitoring, and sound risk management practices to support sustainable earnings growth," it added.
Barring unforeseen circumstances, Apex said the application to Bursa Malaysia in relation to the proposed private placement is expected to be made within one month from the date of this announcement and is expected to be completed by second half of 2026.
M&A Securities has been appointed as the principal adviser and placement agent to the company for the proposed private placement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mitra allocates RM66mil for six Indian community socioeconomic programmes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has allocated RM65.5 million to roll out six key socioeconomic development initiatives spanning health, education, and entrepreneurship.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the programmes, which are expected to
benefit
at least 50,000 recipients, were made possible following Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's announcement of an additional RM50 million allocation on May 10.
Among the key initiatives is the expansion of the dialysis assistance subsidy under the Arogya Madani programme for the Indian community in the B40 and M40 categories, targeting 1,300 chronic kidney disease patients.
"We will allocate RM5,000 per patient for one year. This is roughly RM200 per dialysis session for a maximum of 25 sessions.
"The target group is 1,300 chronic kidney disease patients, with a total allocation of RM6.5 million," Ramanan said at a press conference on the first phase of the initiatives here today.
He said Mitra previously received only RM5 million for such support. Applications will open on June 5 through the Mitra website.
Under the RM10 million Celik Madani preschool programme, a subsidy of up to RM2,530 will be provided to benefit 4,000 children from B40 Indian households.
Ramanan said this would be the final year funds are channelled directly to kindergartens before transitioning to direct payouts to parents. This is to address previous issues where some parents unknowingly made double payments or were unaware of the financial aid.
For secondary school students, the Kalvi Madani tuition programme has been allocated RM5 million to benefit 5,000 B40 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) and Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) students. Registered tuition centres under the Education Ministry are invited to submit requests for proposals on June 5 to qualify for participation. The previous allocation for the programme was between RM1 million and RM2 million.
Mitra has also increased allocations for partnerships with parliamentary constituencies from RM100,000 to RM150,000.
However, Mitra will withhold the 2026 allocations from several members of parliament who have yet to submit their performance reports for the 2023 programmes, as periodic audits alongside integrity bodies will be strictly enforced.
"There are several MPs who have yet to submit their reports for the 2023 programmes. Until I receive these reports, we will not disburse the 2026 Mitra funds. Reports are absolutely mandatory for all Mitra programmes.
"As discussed with the acting secretary-general and Mitra director-general, we will conduct periodic monitoring. All involved agencies, including the Integrity Unit and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), will monitor all ongoing programmes," he said.
The fifth initiative, Thunai Madani, is a collaboration with Yayasan Kebajikan Negara aimed at providing one-off financial aid of up to RM5,000 to 10,000 B40 Indian individuals and families struggling with post-crisis living costs.
Evaluated on a case-by-case basis, the programme is structured into four packages: disaster care, health and wellness, community support, and family care.
The packages provide targeted financial assistance for various essential needs, including RM1,000 in disaster emergency aid, RM3,000 for autism early intervention, and RM1,000 each for maternity and marriage assistance.
The sixth initiative, Uyirgu Madani, offers start-up grants of up to RM50,000 to 500 B40 and M40 Indian micro, small, and medium enterprises. The grants will be disbursed in phases upon successful submission of progress reports and monitoring by Mitra and its Integrity Unit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stepfather faces five charges of rape, sexual assault against teen stepdaughter
Article:
MUAR: A teenage girl's decision to lodge a police report led to her stepfather being charged in the magistrate's court here today with five counts of rape and physical sexual assault against her since 2022.
The 47-year-old plantation worker pleaded guilty to four counts of physical sexual assault before magistrate Arthana Sarma Rao, but claimed trial to the rape charge.
On the first, second, third, and fifth charges, the accused was charged under Section 14 (a), (b), and (d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 for committing physical sexual assault against the teenager, who is now 15.
The section provides for a prison sentence of up to 20 years and whipping upon conviction for each charge.
On the fourth charge, the man was accused of raping her under Section 376 (1) of the Penal Code, which carries a jail sentence of up to 20 years and whipping.
The offences were committed at a house in Segamat between 10pm and midnight, across 2022, April 2025, and most recently in May.
According to the police report, the victim decided to take action as she could no longer tolerate the accused's actions.
She claimed the abuse had been going on for several years, but she was forced to keep it a secret from her mother due to threats made by the accused.
Following the report, police arrested the suspect on May 24.
Deputy public prosecutor Diyana Najihah Muhamed Fauzi appeared for the prosecution while the accused was unrepresented.
The court set June 3 for the presentation of facts and sentencing for the sexual assault charges, and for document submission regarding the rape charge at the Sessions Court.
No bail was offered as the accused pleaded guilty to the sexual assault charges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Sabah's 40 per cent rights to be clarified tomorrow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Sabah's 40 per cent rights entitlement is expected to be clarified tomorrow.
"The 40 per cent Sabah rights issue will be explained tomorrow in Sabah," he said after performing prayers at the Saidina Hamzah Mosque in Batu today.
He was responding to a question on the Special Grant rate for Sabah.
Yesterday, GRS remained firm on its stance that a revised Special Grant rate for Sabah under Articles 112C and 112D of the Federal Constitution must be implemented this year.
GRS secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said that despite ongoing legal proceedings at the Court of Appeal over the state's 40 per cent entitlement, he is confident the federal government would agree to review and increase the Special Grant rate in 2026.
MORE TO COME...
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wong Chen questions sudden, 'uncourteous' removal as MDV chairman
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen questioned his sudden removal as non-executive chairman of Malaysia Debt Ventures Bhd, following what he described as improvements in the company's performance.
Wong said his tenure at MDV would officially end May 31, but he had not received any formal letter informing him of the decision, only a note to the legal department instructing that he be removed by this Sunday.
He said the previous chairman appointed by Khairy Jamaluddin was allowed to complete his term even after the change of government.
"My friend sent me a message saying that, in his view, my termination was done in a manner that was uncourteous.
"In this case, a non-executive chairman who helped steer the company back into shape with the support of my team and protected it from undue influence was given just five days' notice," he said at a press conference here today.
Wong, who was appointed MDV non-executive chairman effective May 23, 2023, said the government-linked financial institution had been struggling financially when he first joined.
"When I first joined, it was losing money. I won't say I did a lot of work, but I ensured governance was in place and that the organisation was not corrupt.
"A bank must make money. That is the point of a bank and we have recovered.
"In my first full year as chairman in 2024, we recorded RM3.5 million. For 2025, I cannot give exact figures yet, but it is more than double RM3.5 million.
"We are on track to make close to RM11 million to RM12 million this year. The performance was progressing really well," he said.
On Wednesday, Wong revealed that his tenure was
abruptly cut short by the Finance Ministry
.
Wong said he was informed by MDV management just before chairing a board meeting at 10am yesterday that they had received a letter from the ministry.
The letter instructed the management to terminate his non-executive chairmanship immediately, effective May 31.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke calls for tougher laws against cable theft
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Transport Minister Anthony Loke has called for tougher legislation against cable theft.
"The Home Ministry must strengthen existing laws to deter those who threaten the safety of the country's public transport network," he said at a press conference at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station today.
He said the Transport Ministry had already raised the matter with the Home Ministry and would continue to push for firmer action.
"We have told the Home Ministry that several improvements are needed to existing acts and laws to ensure stricter action against cable thieves. The existing laws need to be enhanced to allow firmer action," he said.
He said the matter remained under the Home Ministry's purview, but the Transport Ministry would continue to engage with the ministry on further action.
"The rail system in Kuala Lumpur and the Klang Valley is the pulse of our public transport. The reliability and safety of the rail system must be in the best possible state," he said.
Yesterday, An Ampang/Sri Petaling Line train derailed while passing through a track switch at the
Chan Sow Lin Station
, causing widespread service disruptions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man charged with trafficking four people, including three minors, for sex exploitation
Article:
SANDAKAN: A 32-year-old man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with trafficking four people, including three underage girls, for sexual exploitation.
No plea was recorded from Chu Nyuk Sheng after the charges were read before magistrate Nur Faezah Jafry.
Chu faces three charges of trafficking minors for sexual exploitation, involving two 15-year-olds, one a Malaysian and the other a foreigner, and a 17-year-old foreigner.
These charges were framed under Section 14(1) of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007 (Act 670), which carries a sentence of life imprisonment or a minimum of five years in jail, and whipping upon conviction.
For the fourth charge, he was accused of trafficking a 30-year-old foreign woman for sexual exploitation under Section 12 of Act 670, which provides for a jail term of up to 20 years and a fine upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Ong Teik Yi appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court denied bail for the accused and set June 15 for mention at the Sessions Court.
According to the facts of the case, a team of police officers from the Criminal Investigation Department raided a hotel in Bandar Sandakan around 10.45pm on May 14, where they arrested the suspect and rescued the four victims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Just you wait': Man fined RM1,500 for threatening Perkeso officer
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A car wash owner was fined RM1,500 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to obstructing a Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) officer from discharging his duties last week.
Abdul Azim A. Karim, 37, made the plea after the charge was read out before magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman.
Azim was accused of raising his voice and accused the 32-year-old male Perkeso inspector of conducting an inspection before telling him, "I remember your face, you wait".
The offence was committed at a car wash premises in Jalan Parit Jalil here at 2.50pm on May 19.
He was charged under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant from carrying out his duties.
Based on the facts of the case, the complainant and two other Perkeso inspectors were conducting a routine check on employee contributions at the premises.
While the complainant was inspecting the back of the premises, the accused became dissatisfied and claimed the inspection was carried out without his permission.
At the same time, the accused spoke harshly and uttered the threatening words to the officer.
Deputy public prosecutor Sharifah Natasha Syed Ahmad prosecuted, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court also ordered the accused to serve three months in prison if he failed to pay the fine. The accused paid the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perlis MB: I shot rampaging cow to protect the public
Article:
KANGAR: Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah has clarified that he was forced to shoot a sacrificial cow to prevent the animal from injuring volunteers and members of the public.
Abu Bakar explained the incident, which occurred during the Kuala Perlis State Constituency Perdana Korban Programme, to clear up public misunderstandings following a viral video of the event.
He said the shot was only fired at the leg of the cow after its restraint rope snapped, before the slaughtering process was carried out as usual.
"We organised a sacrificial programme involving 25 cows. Only one managed to break free, run off, and run amok, so we had to act to avoid any harm or danger to the staff or the public.
"The cow did not die from the shot; the bullet hit its leg, and we then slaughtered it normally. I personally slaughtered 24 cows, and this was the only one we had to shoot in the leg before slaughtering," he said.
The Kuala Perlis assemblyman added that the swift action was a precautionary measure, noting that there have been recent fatal incidents involving sacrificial animals running amok in other states.
"I shot it to prevent undesirable incidents like those that occurred in several other locations across the country, where some cases even resulted in the loss of lives due to being gored by cows," he said.
The incident follows recent reports of sacrificial animals running amok during the slaughtering process, including two separate buffalo attacks in Ulu Tiram, Johor, which left one man dead from severe leg injuries and another seriously injured with a fractured rib.
Meanwhile, police confirmed they have yet to receive any report regarding the viral video, though preliminary investigations are underway.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said authorities are aware of the footage circulating on social media.
The viral video showed Abu Bakar discharging a shotgun at a cow while leading the sacrificial slaughter at Dataran Lok 9 here.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Review rail governance to restore public trust after LRT disruption, says advocate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The recent LRT train service disruption should prompt a broader review of rail reliability governance, said MY Mobility Vision founder and senior adviser Wan Agyl Wan Hassan.
He said Malaysia should move beyond a project-centric transport mindset.
"This latest incident should therefore become more than just another temporary news cycle. It should become a catalyst for a broader national conversation about rail reliability governance," he said, responding to the disruption on the
Ampang and Sri Petaling Light Rail Transit (LRT) lines
yesterday, triggered by a signalling system failure near Chan Sow Lin station.
Wan Agyl said the country must place "equal emphasis on operational resilience, maintenance discipline, and asset lifecycle sustainability" to ensure the long-term reliability of its rail network.
"First, a transparent technical investigation involving independent rail systems and signalling experts is critical, not merely to determine the direct cause of the incident," he said.
He said the exercise would also strengthen public confidence that lessons from the disruption would be identified and acted upon properly.
He also called on authorities to assess other critical switch and interlocking zones across the network, warning that failures involving such components could have wider operational and safety implications.
He said greater transparency on reliability performance, maintenance modernisation efforts, asset renewal progress, and service recovery plans would also be needed to rebuild public trust.
"Global experience shows that transparency itself helps rebuild commuter confidence," he said.
He added that future rail expansion plans must give greater weight to long-term maintenance and operational sustainability, including lifecycle funding, engineering workforce capability, spare parts readiness and resilience planning.
"Building infrastructure is only the first phase of nation-building. Sustaining infrastructure safely and reliably for decades is the real test of institutional maturity," he said.
Wan Agyl said commuters deserved clear answers on service restoration timelines, ongoing inspections and safeguards being implemented during the recovery process.
"Modern transport systems are not judged solely by whether incidents occur.
"They are judged by how transparently they communicate, how quickly they recover, how seriously they investigate, and whether they learn fast enough to prevent recurrence," he said.
He added that Malaysia needed not only more transport projects, but transport systems that commuters could depend on daily.
"Once commuters begin questioning whether the system is dependable, the issue is no longer just about transport. It becomes about confidence in public institutions themselves," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke pledges to take full responsibility for Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Transport Minister Anthony Loke has pledged to take full responsibility for the Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment.
"I will not shy away from accountability even though the incident was deeply disappointing," he said at a press conference at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station today.
Loke said an independent special task force had been established to conduct a thorough
investigation
into the cause of the derailment and that the necessary action would follow.
"I am responsible for taking the necessary action. That is the responsibility I must bear. If I were to step down every time an incident occurs, that is not how it works. Responsibility means ensuring the right actions are taken," he said.
He said the government's support for Prasarana would continue and that the Transport Ministry had been providing assistance to the rail operator all along.
"Our support for Prasarana is continuous. Whatever support the Transport Ministry can provide has been given all this while. But incidents like this are deeply disappointing, and we will take whatever action is necessary to ensure the situation is safe," he said.
Services on the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line are expected to resume fully on Wednesday, June 3.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OCK posts RM11.3mil Q3 profit on RM174.7mil revenue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: OCK Group Bhd posted a net profit of RM11.25 million on revenue of RM174.7 million for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26).
For the nine-month period, the telecommunications network provider recorded a net profit of RM27.36 million on revenue of RM499.9 million.
Following the change of the company's financial year end from Dec 31, 2024, to June 30, 2025, no comparable figures for the previous corresponding period are available.
OCK group managing director Datuk Sam Ooi Chin Khoon said the earnings reflect the company's strengthening position within the telecommunications and digital infrastructure ecosystem.
He said the performance was supported by the contributions from the telecommunication network services projects alongside the increasing participation in data centre-related activities.
It was further boosted by the continued resilience of the recurring income base through the regional tower leasing and managed services portfolio across Southeast Asia.
"As demand for connectivity, fiberisation, digital infrastructure and 5G network deployment continues to accelerate across the region, we remain optimistic on the industry's long-term outlook and believe OCK is well-positioned to capitalise on these structural growth trends," he said in a statement.
Ooi added that the successful listing of its energy solutions arm, EI Power Bhd, marked a key milestone for OCK, unlocking value in its power solutions segment while strengthening the company's platform for future expansion.
"Given the exciting developments, we remain focused on enhancing operational execution, expanding our infrastructure footprint and capturing new opportunities across telecommunications, digital solutions and sustainable energy initiatives in order to drive sustainable long-term growth and deliver better value creation for all our stakeholders," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Decomposing body found wrapped in mattress in shophouse
Article:
MELAKA: A potential tenant looking to rent a shophouse in Kampung Padang, Pokok Mangga here, discovered a decomposing body wrapped in a mattress yesterday.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said police received a report at about 7pm from the premises owner, who had gone to inspect the property with the person.
Upon arrival, the potential tenant noticed a foul stench and spotted a suspicious bundle in the living room, prompting them to contact the authorities.
A police team dispatched to the scene discovered the body wrapped inside a folded mattress.
"So far, the identity and gender of the victim have yet to be determined due to the advanced state of decomposition. We believe the victim died between two weeks and a month ago.
"The body was found with the hands bound and wrapped inside the mattress," he said.
Dzulkhairi was speaking to the media after witnessing the handover of duties for the Management Department head and the Melaka Tengah district police chief at the Malim police station open hall here today.
He said preliminary investigations indicated foul play based on the condition of the scene.
Following this, police arrested a man in his 40s, believed to be connected to the victim, to assist in investigations.
"A urine test conducted on the suspect returned positive for methamphetamine, and he has seven prior criminal offences," he said.
Dzulkhairi added that the unit was originally rented by a woman before being occupied by her son, who is the suspect.
"When arrested, the suspect was still under the influence of drugs and could not fully cooperate with the police. A detailed investigation is underway from all angles, including determining the motive and the relationship between the victim and the suspect," he said.
The suspect has been remanded for seven days starting today to assist in investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bull turns on former owner in Kluang attack
Article:
KLUANG: A 65-year-old man was injured after being gored by a raging bull at Kampung Murni Jaya, Layang-Layang, near here, on Tuesday.
The 10am incident occurred after the man, the previous owner of the animal, rushed to the village following a call from the buyer informing him that the livestock had become aggressive.
The man had earlier sold the bull to another villager.
Upon arriving at the location, he attempted to calm and restrain the animal, but it suddenly charged at him, striking him near the left armpit and chest.
He was rushed to the Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom Hospital and was warded for a day.
He has since been discharged and is in stable condition.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh confirmed the incident but noted that no police report had been lodged.
The incident follows a tragic case yesterday where a 45-year-old man died after being gored by a buffalo while assisting in sacrificial ritual preparations in Kampung Tenang, Ulu Tiram.
The victim initially managed to walk to a four-wheel-drive vehicle before collapsing.
He later succumbed to his injuries at the Sultanah Aminah Hospital due to severe vascular injuries to his left leg.
Police have classified the case as
sudden death
.
In another incident, a 45-year-old man in Kampung Sungai Tiram, Ulu Tiram, was injured after an aggressive buffalo attacked him. He survived the encounter but suffered a fractured left rib.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bakery products maker HSS Holdings to raise RM13.5mil from IPO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bakery products manufacturer HSS Holdings Bhd, en route to a listing on Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market, aims to raise RM13.5 million through its initial public offering (IPO).
The company said the IPO comprises a public issue of 75 million new shares and an offer for sale of 52.5 million existing shares, priced at 18 sen per share. The listing is scheduled for June 23, 2026.
Of the total proceeds, RM2.57 million will be utilised for capital expenditure for the group's manufacturing facilities; RM4.50 million for the repayment of bank borrowings; RM2.93 million for working capital; and RM3.50 million for the defrayment of listing expenses, it said in a statement.
Commenting on the expansion plan, HSS production director Joshua Lau Ern Tien said the group intends to expand its automation machinery ahead of Dec 31, 2030, in line with the incentive announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during the tabling of Budget 2026 last year.
Under Budget 2026, existing companies undertaking expansion projects will be entitled to a 100 per cent income tax exemption for five years, with the application period extended until Dec 31, 2030.
"We are expanding both our manufacturing facilities with more automated lines...this is going to help us to increase more than double of our current capacity.
"Currently, our utilisation rate averages about 70 per cent and we hope to increase this further," he told a press conference after the company's prospectus launch here, today.
HSS is principally involved in the sourcing of bakery products, trading of bakery and other products, as well as the manufacturing of bakery products.
With business roots dating back to 2004, the group has built more than 20 years of operating experience in the bakery products market and established a broad market presence through its extensive network of wholesalers, distributors and retailers, offering more than 6,000 products across bakeries and other products.
Lau also said the company plans to venture into the e-commerce segment by fourth quarter of this year to enhance brand awareness as it currently relies on wholesalers, retailers and distributors for its sales channels.
Today, HSS's network comprises 330 wholesalers, 117 retailers and five distributors in Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Australia, Cambodia, and South Korea.
"This is part of our plan for the brand's awareness as the younger generation are more active online. We want people to know that we are still very active in the market," he said.
Upon listing, HSS will have a market capitalisation of RM90 million, based on the IPO price of 18 sen per share, with an enlarged issued share capital of 500 million shares.
M & A Securities Sdn Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter, and placement agent for the IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Laos transitions from backpackers haven to global coffee producer
Article:
PHNOM PENH: Lao farmers are changing coffee's nickname "black gold" into a golden industry, redefining the rural economy that feeds nearly 300,000 workers in the mountainous regions.
Diligent farmers are transforming the Lao coffee industry into a multimillion-dollar sector that mostly grows Robusta coffee and some Arabica varieties in the southern Bolaven Plateau.
Last weekend, along with France and the European Union (EU), Laos launched the 2026 International Tea and Coffee Day themed "Two Brews, One Culture" in Vientiane.
"With the unique quality of Lao coffee and tea increasingly in demand globally, this festival served as a powerful platform for networking and expanding market access for local producers," Agriculture and Environment Minister Dr Linkham Douangsavanh said at the event, as reported by the Vientiane Timeson Thursday.
Laos policymakers are positioning coffee as a strategic crop, not only to generate revenue but also as a sector to create jobs for the country's young demographic of about 7.9 million people.
The landlocked nation exported over 50,000 tonnes of coffee worth about US$100 million (RM397 million) in 2024, according to Vientiane Times.
As Laos plans to exit the Least Developed Country status by the end of 2027, the emerging coffee sector will be critical to propel its economy, which largely depends on electricity exports and its tourism sector.
Last year, tourism raked in US$1.4 billion (RM6 billion) with about four million international tourist arrivals, while electricity sales to neighbouring countries like Cambodia, Thailand, and Vietnam generated about US$2 billion (RM8 billion).
The Food and Agriculture Organisation of the United Nations (FAO) describes coffee as one of the most widely consumed beverages across the world, and one of the most globally traded commodities.
The largest coffee-producing countries are Brazil, Vietnam, and Colombia, while the EU and the United States (US) are the largest consuming and importing markets.
Annual revenues from the global coffee industry amount to US$200 billion (RM793 billion), according to the organisation.
Coffee cultivation has also played a pivotal role in replacing opium poppy farming among rural communities in Houaphanh Province.
With support from the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC), the government of Laos successfully encouraged farmers to abandon poppy cultivation and transition to coffee.
About 900 families have now benefited from the sustainable alternative livelihoods project that has been providing a steady income since 2015 in the mountainous northeastern Houaphanh Province.
Laos coffee exports reach about 20 international markets.
At the event, EU Ambassador to Laos Mark Gallagher said the EU is one of the world's largest import markets for tea, highlighting another opportunity for Lao farmers.
"European consumers are increasingly seeking authentic, organic, and sustainably sourced products. Lao tea, with its rich identity and unique character, is well positioned to benefit from this growing demand."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IJN's Dr Jeswant becomes first Malaysian elected to prestigious global thoracic surgery body
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: For more than three decades, Professor Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Jeswant Dillon has spent countless hours in operating theatres handling some of Malaysia's most complex cardiothoracic surgery cases, while balancing the demands of clinical practice, academia, research and mentorship.
Earlier this month, the senior consultant cardiothoracic surgeon at Institut Jantung Negara (IJN) added another milestone to his distinguished career when he became the first Malaysian elected as a member of the prestigious American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS), placing Malaysia on the global cardiothoracic surgery map.
Founded in 1917, the AATS is regarded as the world's leading organisation in the development of cardiothoracic surgical skills and innovations in patient care.
Its membership is highly selective, comprising internationally recognised surgeons who have demonstrated sustained excellence and significant contributions to the field of cardiothoracic surgery.
For Dr Jeswant, the recognition represents more than a professional accolade, reflecting his unwavering commitment and dedication to advancing cardiothoracic surgery both in Malaysia and internationally.
"The award is a recognition of years of sacrifice invested in the discipline, representing decades of training, long hours both in clinical practice and academia and sustained discipline. It feels less like a title that is bestowed but more as an acknowledgement that the sacrifices mattered," he said in an interview.
A graduate of Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Dr Jeswant received his initial cardiothoracic training at IJN before pursuing an advanced fellowship at the renowned Mayo Clinic in the United States, laying the foundation for a career that would position him among the region's leading cardiothoracic surgeons.
Over the years, he has held several key leadership roles at IJN, including head of the Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery from 2014 to 2019.
His clinical expertise spans valve repair and replacement, minimally invasive cardiac surgery, coronary artery bypass surgery, corrective procedures for anomalous coronary artery and hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy (HOCM), as well as video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS).
Beyond the operating theatre, Dr Jeswant has also carved a strong presence in academia, education and training, serving as adjunct professor at Universitas Airlangga in Indonesia and Universiti Malaya, as well as Chairman of the Speciality Subcommittee for Education (SSC-EDU) Cardiothoracic Surgery for the Malaysian Medical Council (MMC) and Board Director of IJN University College (IJNUC).
His active participation in academia includes contributions as an author and invited editorial commentator for leading peer-reviewed, high-impact international medical journals, as well as a speaker at scientific conferences.
Currently serving as Sports and Fitness Ambassador at IJN, he has also been involved in various sports medicine initiatives, including serving as team doctor for the national cycling contingent to the Paris Olympics 2024 and as a member of the Medical Committee of the Southeast Asian Games Federation (SEAGF) and the Medical and Anti-Doping Committee of the Olympic Council of Malaysia (OCM).
Despite the international acclaim, Dr Jeswant views his election to the AATS not merely as a personal achievement but as an opportunity to strengthen Malaysia's presence within the global cardiothoracic community.
"I believe this recognition creates an important opportunity to strengthen ties between the AATS and IJN through deeper academic exchange, research and clinical collaboration, as well as regional leadership in cardiothoracic care.
> Sharmini_NST:
"IJN has developed substantial expertise in complex cardiovascular and thoracic care within Southeast Asia, while AATS represents one of the world's leading platforms for surgical innovation, education, research and leadership," he said.
As president of the Malaysian Association for Thoracic and Cardiovascular Surgery (MATCVS), Dr Jeswant hopes the recognition will open wider doors for younger Malaysian surgeons through mentorship, fellowships, international collaborations and specialised training opportunities.
He believes stronger academic and research networks will enable Malaysia to contribute more actively to global advancements in cardiovascular and thoracic care, particularly as the field evolves towards minimally invasive therapies, artificial intelligence, precision medicine and tissue-based innovations.
"By being the first recipient of this award from Malaysia, it has placed Malaysia on the global cardiothoracic landscape. I hope this recognition by the AATS can help create greater visibility and pave the way for future Malaysian cardiothoracic specialists and trainees on the international stage," he added.
For Dr Jeswant, however, the future of cardiothoracic surgery must remain patient-centred, ensuring that advanced and high-quality care becomes increasingly accessible to Malaysians nationwide.
He said that through stronger collaboration between institutions, professional societies and international organisations, Malaysia is well-positioned to emerge as a respected leader in cardiothoracic surgery within the region and beyond.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Form Four boy held for supplying drug-laced vape to girlfriend
Article:
MELAKA: Police have detained a Form Four schoolboy suspected of supplying drug-laced vapes to his 14-year-old girlfriend.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 17-year-old was arrested yesterday and has been remanded for five days to assist in investigations.
He said police are still conducting further investigations, including tracking down the source of the vape supply believed to have been obtained by the suspect.
"So far, we are still investigating where the student obtained the vape supply. However, the suspect's urine test returned negative for drugs," he said when met here today.
The case is being investigated under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 for elements of negligence or neglect, and Section 376 of the Penal Code for rape.
It was reported yesterday that a 14-year-old girl, suspected of being under the influence of drug-laced vape, slit her throat and wrists after being barred by her foster mother from meeting her boyfriend at a house in Jonker Walk here.
Dzulkhairi said the incident, which occurred at 10.33pm on Sunday, was triggered when the victim became enraged after her 49-year-old foster mother stopped her from seeing her 17-year-old boyfriend.
He said the victim then went into a first-floor bedroom of the house and inflicted injuries upon herself with a knife.
"The foster mother claimed the teenager was distressed before the incident.
"The victim was sent to Malacca Hospital for treatment in the Emergency Unit and
reported
to be stable," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Journalists on frontline in defending information integrity – Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said journalists must remain on the front line in defending the integrity of information at a time when slander can spread instantly, and perception often outweighs reality.
"The role of journalists is not confined to recording events, but also acts as an important voice in upholding the truth when society is confronted with confusion and speculation," he said in a Facebook post in conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) today.
He said every report requires scrutiny, verification, and a strong sense of responsibility, as public trust is not easily earned but is built on honesty, transparency, and a consistent commitment.
"When integrity is upheld as a guiding principle and truth is placed as the highest priority, journalists will continue to stand as a bulwark of credible information," he said.
"Ultimately, journalism is not only about reporting the news, but about preserving trust and safeguarding credibility," he added.
Meanwhile, the Works Ministry also conveyed its appreciation to members of the media fraternity who continue to deliver credible, trustworthy and integrity-driven reporting.
It said ethical journalism plays an important role in strengthening national unity, harmony and stability.
"Fair and fact-based reporting can enhance public awareness of current issues and contribute towards shaping a mature and well-informed society," the ministry said in a Facebook post.
The ministry also expressed hope that the journalism profession would continue to be strengthened as a pillar of national development in line with the aspirations of Malaysia Madani.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops probe viral video of Perlis MB  shooting sacrificial cow with shotgun
Article:
KANGAR: Police have confirmed they have yet to receive a report regarding a viral video allegedly showing Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah shooting a sacrificial cow with a shotgun, though a preliminary investigation into the incident is underway.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police are aware of the video recording, which has been circulating on social media since yesterday.
"The incident is already within the knowledge of the police. As of now, no police report has been received, but an initial investigation is being carried out," he said when contacted.
Yusharifuddin said a detailed investigation is being conducted to identify the type of ammunition and weapon used in the incident.
He added that police will record a statement from the menteri besar soon to assist in further investigations.
"Whether live ammunition was used or not is still under investigation. We will call the menteri besar to obtain a statement regarding this issue, he said.
Earlier, a video recording went viral alleging that the Perlis menteri besar shot a cow using a shotgun for the sacrificial ritual in conjunction with the Aidiladha celebration.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC examining documents tied to Bukit Jalil National Stadium upgrade
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Upgrading works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil are under scrutiny following a Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) probe into the procurement process.
The MACC confirmed it received a report regarding the evaluation and implementation process of the upgrading works, as reported by a local news portal yesterday.
The commission said it is scrutinising all related information and documents.
"The MACC always views any allegations involving elements of corruption, abuse of power and misconduct involving public interest and government procurement," it said in a statement.
The graft busters welcomed cooperation from any party with relevant information to come forward and assist investigations to ensure a comprehensive and professional inquiry.
Yesterday, Free Malaysia Today reported that the RM24 million upgrade project at the stadium had come under scrutiny after a contractor lodged a report with the MACC.
The complaint centred on two tenders linked to preparations for the 2027 SEA Games, involving a new running track and a protective flooring system used during concerts and large-scale events.
The contractor reportedly lodged the report at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya on Tuesday, raising concerns over the selection of a firm's prefabricated track system.
According to the report, several international brands used at Olympic and world-level events had participated in the tender process, with shortlisted bids reportedly priced between RM14 million and RM15 million.
The complaint questioned why the firm was selected despite other established systems being offered at similar prices, and raised concerns over the evaluation of durability, competition history and long-term value.
It also cited World Athletics records showing that the firm currently has only one certified 2025 Class 1 facility at the Beiyuan Track and Field Stadium in Chongqing, China.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OSK Q1 net profit rises 11pct to RM138.04mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: OSK Holdings Bhd posted a higher net profit in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q FY2026) by 11 pct to RM138.04 million, supported by a stronger operating profit from the property and financial services businesses, and higher investment income from the group's stake in RHB Bank Bhd.
Revenue was also higher by nine per cent to RM434.65 million from RM400.58 million in the same quarter last year, stated the conglomerate in a Bursa Malaysia filing today.
OSK has diversified business interests in property, financial services, industries, hospitality, and investment holding.
During the quarter, operating profit grew 16 per cent to RM80.8 million, while profit from associates rose 10 per cent to RM92.4 million.
The investment holding segment contributed a 13 per cent increase in pre-tax profit to RM83.2 million in 1Q FY2026, compared with 1Q FY2025, primarily attributable to increased earnings from RHB Bank. OSK holds a 10.27 per cent equity interest in RHB Bank.
OSK executive chairman Tan Sri Ong Leong Huat said despite ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties, the company continue to see steady business fundamentals across its property and financial services segments.
"While external uncertainties remain, we believe the group is well-positioned to navigate the evolving operating landscape through prudent management, operational agility, and continued investments in growth opportunities.
"We will continue building on our strong foundation to create sustainable value for our stakeholders over the long term," he said.
Looking ahead, the group remained cautiously optimistic amid ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the impact of the Middle East crisis on construction, logistics, and raw material costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's reserve assets edge higher to US$129.7bil in April
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's official reserve assets rose to US$129.73 billion at the end of April 2026 from US$126.61 billion a month earlier, while other foreign currency assets stood at US$66.6 million, Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) said.
The central bank said pre-determined short-term outflows of foreign currency loans, securities and deposits over the next 12 months totalled US$8.22 billion.
These included scheduled repayments of the government's external borrowings as well as the maturity of foreign currency Bank Negara interbank bills.
"Net short forward positions amounted to US$24.45 billion as at end-April 2026, reflecting the management of ringgit liquidity in the money market.
"In line with the practice adopted since April 2006, the data excludes projected foreign currency inflows arising from interest income and the drawdown of project loans," Bank Negara said.
It said these projected foreign currency inflows amount to US$2.98 billion in the next 12 months.
The central bank said the only contingent short-term net drain on foreign currency assets is government guarantees of foreign currency debt due within one year, amounting to US$846.4 million.
"There are no foreign currency loans with embedded options, no undrawn, unconditional credit lines provided by or to other central banks, international organisations, banks and other financial institutions.
"Bank Negara also does not engage in foreign currency options vis-à-vis the ringgit," it said.
Overall, the central bank said the detailed breakdown of international reserves under the International Monetary Fund's Special Data Dissemination Standard format showed that Malaysia's international reserves remained usable as at end-April 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Top China leaders skip Asia defence summit headlined by US
Article:
SINGAPORE: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth is the headline speaker at Asia's premier defence summit opening Friday, but China's top officials aren't expected despite weighty questions like Taiwan and the war in Iran.
Beijing's defence minister is to skip the three-day Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore for the second year running, which analysts viewed as a sign of China's rising power.
Yet, the forum that brings together top officials from around 45 nations has historically provided a setting for debate as well as quiet and high-profile diplomacy.
Defence Minister Dong Jun's absence means no meeting there with Hegseth as China warns the US over its involvement with Taiwan and Washington seeks an end to the Mideast war.
The Middle East was the source of 57 percent of China's direct seaborne crude imports in 2025 – 5.9 million barrels per day (mbd) – maritime tracking firm Kpler said.
Hegseth's second trip to the Shangri-La Dialogue comes after US President Donald Trump's visit to China in May, and his subsequent suggestion that US arms sales to Taiwan could be used as a bargaining chip with Beijing.
Hegseth's speech on Saturday is expected to be "quite strong against China, but mainly for internal (US) consumption", said Oh Ei Sun, senior fellow at the Singapore Institute of International Affairs.
"I think under Trump anything is negotiable and even with enemies deals can be done... (even) with Taiwan as a negotiating chip," Oh told AFP.
Trump said "fantastic" trade deals were struck after his visit to China, although details were vague and no breakthrough with Beijing emerged in the war with Iran.
CHINA ARRIVED AS 'MAJOR POWER'
As the US and Iran clashed again on Thursday, threatening to derail a fragile push for peace, it "is unlikely that any possible deal will be discussed at the Shangri-La Dialogue", Oh said.
China sent Dong to the dialogue as recently as 2024, where he and then Pentagon chief Lloyd Austin met for their first substantive face-to-face talks in 18 months.
"Dong was absent last year, reportedly due to China's reluctance to engage with... Hegseth," said William Choong, principal fellow at the ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute think-tank.
China said Thursday it would send experts and scholars from its army's study institutions.
Major General Meng Xiangqing of the National Defense University will lead the delegation, which is to include scholars from the National Defense University, the Academy of Military Sciences and the Navy.
Two other former defence ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu previously spoke at Shangri-La. Both were subsequently handed suspended death sentences on graft charges, analysts point out.
"It's kind of a poisoned chalice for any Chinese defence minister to speak out publicly," said Jennifer Parker, adjunct professor at the University of Western Australia's Defence and Security Institute.
With Dong again not attending, one of the reasons seemed obvious, said Choong, writing for the Lowy Institute think-tank.
"For one thing, China has truly arrived as a major power in the region, so it does not really need to send its defence minister to brave a fusillade of questions and try to 'score' brownie points," he said.
Beijing however, like last year, risked not having a senior leader present if the two most pertinent global security issues – Taiwan and the opening of the Strait of Hormuz – do come up.
"At a time when perceptions of US leadership are falling, Beijing could soothe some jangled nerves in the region by reassuring delegates that it would use force against the island only as a last resort," Choong said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor govt urged to explain below-market land sale near forest reserve
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: The Selangor government must explain why 68.4 hectares acres of land near the Ayer Hitam Forest Reserve in Puchong were sold at only a quarter of their market value.
Subang member of parliament Wong Chen said documents obtained through Memorandum of Transfer (MoT) searches revealed that the land was transacted at RM13.80 per sq ft.
However, he said the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN), in calculating the ad valorem duty on May 5, 2019, had valued the land at RM55 per sq ft.
"If we use the LHDN valuation of RM55 per sq ft, the Selangor government should have sold the land to Perano for around RM404 million instead of RM101 million.
"Thus, the Selangor government had in fact sold the land at a heavily discounted value to Perano in 2012 and, despite the court ruling in 2017, then proceeded to complete the deal and transfer the land to Perano in 2019 and 2020," he said at a press conference here today.
Wong said he believed the land was sold at a significant discount for two additional reasons.
Firstly, he said bungalow lots in Puteri 12, located about 1km from the site, are currently being offered at RM350 per sq ft.
He said the figure was staggering compared to the RM13.80 per sq ft paid by Perano for the undeveloped land.
Wong said based on land titles, National Property Information Centre searches and the MoTs, 12.5 ha of the land are leasehold while the remaining 55.8ha are freehold.
"It is also common knowledge that freehold land is 20 per cent higher in value than leasehold land, and yet the menteri besar in his statement gave only one value of RM13.80 per sq ft," he said.
Wong also referred to Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari's statement that the state government would continue to follow MBSJ's position that the land would never be developed for housing due to steep slopes, in line with a 2017 court ruling that the land is too steep for development.
Wong said the Selangor government instead proceeded to complete the deal, received full payment for the land from Perano and transferred ownership of the land in 2019 and 2020.
He said the development plan was also included in the Rancangan Tempatan MBSJ 2035, which was approved by the Selangor state executive council on Nov 3, 2021, and published on May 26, 2022.
"If it is true that the Selangor government is against the development, why didn't the Selangor government immediately, after the court ruling in 2017, cancel the sale and purchase agreement with Perano and return the deposit paid by Perano for the land?
"Give us full transparency. If the government really wants to help us, clean up and clear their name, show us the 2012 sale and purchase agreement. I am sure the assemblyman, as an exco member, Datuk Ng Sze Han, can get that tomorrow if he asks for it," he said.
Wong added that 9,906 people had signed a petition opposing the development of the land.
Amirudin took office as menteri besar on June 19, 2018. The transfer of ownership took place in 2019 and 2020, and the Rancangan Tempatan MBSJ 2035 was approved in 2021.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kazakhstan's Air Astana boosts Asia network as Middle East crisis reshapes transit flows
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Air Astana is accelerating its expansion across key Southeast Asia markets while positioning Kazakhstan as a growing transit hub between Europe and Asia amid shifting traffic flows due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
The Kazakhstan flag carrier said the ongoing conflict in the Gulf had redirected traffic northbound through Kazakhstan.
This drives stronger transit demand and supporting growth across its international network during the first quarter of 2026.
Air Astana Group chief executive officer Ibrahim Canliel said the airline had swiftly redeployed capacity towards stronger-performing markets including China, Southeast Asia and South Korea.
"We are reallocating very effectively and very actively the capacity that has come free from the Middle East.
"A large part of that has gone to Southeast Asia but particularly to China as well as (South) Korea," he said during the group's first-quarter results briefing.
Canliel said the airline group is continuing to evaluate additional destinations in the region including Kuala Lumpur as part of its broader expansion strategy.
"Kuala Lumpur is one of them but it is not part of the destinations that we have disclosed at this time. We are continuously looking at new destinations in addition to thickening the routes that we are operating," he said.
Air Astana Group said in its statement that flights to Doha, Dubai, Jeddah and Madinah were suspended during the first quarter resulting in a 51 per cent decline in Middle East capacity against plan.
The spare capacity was subsequently redeployed to other higher-demand markets including Bangkok, Male, Phu Quoc and Phuket with Air Astana increasing frequencies and deploying larger Boeing B767 aircraft on selected routes.
The airline also expanded frequencies to London, Frankfurt and Istanbul while increasing services to Guangzhou, Sanya, Seoul, Urumqi and Delhi to capitalise on rising east-west transit.
"We saw a 65 per cent increase in international transit traffic during the first quarter, which was driven largely by a 158 per cent increase during the month of March alone."
"Even when the situation normalises, we expect that the new equilibrium point will be significantly different from what we were seeing three months ago," Canliel said.
Air Astana is also expanding its China network as part of its eastward growth strategy.
In March, the carrier launched a new direct Almaty-Shanghai flight operated three times weekly using Airbus A321LR (long range) aircraft.
The Shanghai route expanded the group's China network to six destinations and increased total capacity between Kazakhstan and China to up to 23 weekly flights compared with 11 weekly flights in the first quarter of 2025.
Canliel said China and India remained among Air Astana Group's strongest growth markets with enormous growth opportunities in the two countries.
Air Astana will introduce Astana-Guangzhou flights in June while its low-cost carrier subsidiary, FlyArystan will introduce Almaty-Xian, Aktau-Urumqi and Astana-Urumqi services.
The airline group also plans to expand its network across Central Asia and the Caucasus.
Air Astana plans to operate triple daily flights to Tashkent, 10 weekly flights to Bishkek and double daily flights to Tbilisi during the summer season.
The airline's network expansion comes as it continues growing its fleet despite ongoing engine supply constraints affecting the global aviation sector.
Canliel said the group expects to grow its fleet to 86 aircraft by 2030, comprising 54 Air Astana aircraft and 32 FlyArystan aircraft.
The fleet will consist of 83 Airbus A320 family aircraft and three Boeing B787-9 aircraft.
Air Astana is preparing to introduce its first B787 aircraft later this year with deliveries scheduled for September and November.
"The first of the B787 will come in September of this year. The aircraft will initially operate short-sector flights for crew and engineering familiarisation," Canliel said.
The new aircraft will gradually replace Air Astana's ageing B767 fleet while providing higher capacity and greater operational flexibility.
However, he said that the group continues to face operational challenges linked to ongoing Pratt & Whitney engine issues affecting part of its fleet.
"The Pratt & Whitney engine issue has been frustrating, to say the least. It continues to prevent us from flying as much of our fleet as we would like," Canliel said.
The majority of Air Astana's fleet comprises A320 family aircraft powered by Pratt & Whitney PW1100G engines, which were affected by the manufacturer's global product recall issued in 2023 due to contamination of powdered metal in the engines.
Air Astana said it secured six additional engines this year including four through lease agreements and two purchases to mitigate operational disruptions.
"It constrains our capacity and consequently has an impact on the cost per ASK (available seat kilometre). We remain in vigorous discussions with Pratt & Whitney to ensure that we accelerate engine inductions and engine deliveries," Canliel said.
Despite the operational pressures, Air Astana Group reported stronger revenues during the first quarter ended March 31, 2026.
Total revenue and other income rose 13.2 per cent year-on-year to RM1.3 billion (US$331 million) while the total number of passengers carried for the group remained stable at 1.95 million with an average load factor of 83.3 per cent.
However, earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortisation and rent/restructuring fell 19.6 per cent to RM191 million (US$48.2 million).
The group posted a net loss of RM83.6 million (US$-21.1 million) for the quarter compared with a net loss of RM28.9 million (US$-7.3 million) in the same quarter in 2025.
Air Astana chief financial officer Gonçalo Pires said March delivered strong revenue performance although cost pressures remained significant.
"Cost pressures on labour, ownership, engineering and traffic drove the CASK (cost per available seat kilometre) numbers up around 20 per cent," he said.
Pires added that the group expects profitability to improve as more aircraft returned to service.
"Addressing the Pratt & Whitney issue will revert this trend. We believe these levels do not reflect the structural cost advantage that the group has in the industry," he said,
Canliel added that the group remains confident in its medium-term outlook despite geopolitical uncertainty and industry-wide supply chain constraints.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RHB Bank Q1 profit rises 14.2pct on stronger income, credit quality
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Bank Bhd recorded a 14.2 per cent increase in net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), driven by steady income growth, disciplined cost management and improved credit quality.
Net profit rose to RM856.75 million from RM750.03 million in the corresponding quarter last year, its Bursa Malaysia filing showed.
Revenue, however, slipped 2.7 per cent to RM4.26 billion compared with RM4.38 billion a year earlier.
No dividend was declared for the quarter.
The bank's total income amounted to RM2.2 billion, supported by contributions from both fund-based and non-fund-based income streams.
Its net fund-based income rose 4.6 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM1.6 billion, driven by 6.2 per cent YoY growth in gross loans and lower funding costs, while net interest margin, supported by liability management, stood at 1.91 per cent.
Meanwhile, RHB's non-fund-based income increased 14.1 per cent YoY to RM0.6 billion, boosted by stronger fee income as well as higher trading and investment income.
The bank said it remains committed to disciplined execution while proactively positioning itself to capture emerging growth opportunities across its operating footprint.
Group managing director and group chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Rashid Mohamad said the operating environment continues to face challenges arising from global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions, shifting trade dynamics and cautious market sentiment.
He said despite these headwinds, the group remains focused on supporting its customers while upholding prudent financial management.
"We recognise that many businesses, particularly small and medium enterprises (SMEs), continue to navigate cost pressures and external uncertainties.
"We are supporting SMEs through targeted measures, including access to financing under Bank Negara Malaysia's SME Stabilisation Relief Facility, supported by guaranteed schemes to enhance funding accessibility," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Storm warning out for Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan, Johor, Sarawak, Sabah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for seven states today, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 7.00 pm.
In the northern and eastern regions of the peninsula, the warning covers Sik and Baling in Kedah; Besut and Setiu in Terengganu; and a large portion of Kelantan, including Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Bachok, Machang, and Pasir Puteh.
Central and southern states are also affected, specifically Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin in Negeri Sembilan, alongside Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, and Kota Tinggi in Johor.
In East Malaysia, MetMalaysia has flagged several divisions in Sarawak, namely Sibu, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau), and Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri, and Marudi).
Meanwhile, in Sabah, the alert extends to the Interior (Tambunan), the West Coast, Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran, and Sandakan), and Kudat (Kota Marudu).
According to the department, such warnings are issued when there are indications of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm/hour that are either imminent or expected to last for more than an hour.
These alerts are short-term and remain valid for a period not exceeding six hours from the time of issuance.
Residents in the affected areas are advised to remain vigilant and take necessary precautions against potential flash floods or falling trees due to strong winds during this period.

Title: ‘This is dangerous’: Anwar warns Malaysians against mistaking social media snippets for knowledge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that children are losing the habit of reading as social media shortens attention spans and replaces knowledge with bite-sized information.
Launching the 2026 National Book Fair at the World Trade Centre here, Anwar who professed himself a bookworm, urged schools, teachers and parents to reignite interest in reading to build a more informed society.
“The reading culture has become a problem in the modern world,” he said.
“Many are no longer interested to read because they only consume information, and much of it from social media. Trying to solve complex problems in just 20 words. Insulting in just 40 words. This is dangerous.
“This is not knowledge. This is not the tradition of knowledge,” heb added.
Anwar said a society cannot “be truly great” without a strong culture of reading and intellectual curiosity.
Recent estimates place Malaysians’ reading average at about five books a year, compared to 6.7 books in Singapore and 6.3 books in Thailand, although the National Library of Malaysia’s broader measure that includes digital reading puts the figure at 24 books annually.
Regionally, Malaysians spend an average of 122 hours a year reading, slightly behind Vietnam’s 123 hours and well below Singapore’s 155 hours.
The Anwar government introduced the RM100 Madani book voucher for secondary in 2024 and expanded it to include university students in public institutions the following year to bolster reading among young people.
Anwar said today the programme would continue and would not be affected by spending cuts.

Title: Missing witness sinks case against two men accused of possessing 150kg of drugs
Article:
SHAH ALAM, May 29 — Two former steamboat restaurant workers were acquitted and freed by the High Court here today after a judge ruled the prosecution failed to secure a key witness sought by the defence in a case involving nearly 150kg of methamphetamine and heroin.
Chan Shi Wei, 29, and Lim Chan Hau, 30, had spent six years in remand over the charges linked to a house in Kota Damansara.
“This failure placed the defence in a disadvantageous position,” Judicial Commissioner Datuk Bhupindar Singh was quoted as saying by news outlet
Free Malaysia Today (FMT)
in the verdict this afternoon.
The judge said the defence had repeatedly made clear it required the witness’s testimony, but the prosecution only attempted to trace the individual at the eleventh hour despite earlier informing the court the witness was still in the country.
Bhupindar also found the prosecution failed to effectively challenge key aspects of the defence during cross-examination.
He also ruled there were serious doubts over whether the two men had exclusive custody and control of the drugs to support the amended possession charge.
According to
FMT
, Chan and Lim were charged with trafficking 51.5kg of methamphetamine, or syabu, and 98.25kg of heroin.
The offences were allegedly committed at a house along Jalan Sepah Puteri 5/2 in Kota Damansara, Petaling Jaya, at about 10am on October 5, 2020.
Chan testified that he had gone to the house on his employer’s instructions to collect detergent and bottled water for the restaurant.
Lim maintained the same account throughout the trial, while a raiding officer confirmed detergent and bottled water were found at the premises.
Chan also told the court he was not a tenant of the house and was arrested in the living room, while the drugs were discovered in one of the rooms.
Lim said he was detained by another police team at a security guard post about 100 metres from the house.
The defence lawyer for Chan were Amirul Jamaluddin, while Lim was represented by Damien Chan, Joshua Kevin and Ian Hannibal.

Title: RM24m Bukit Jalil stadium upgrade project under MACC scrutiny after complaints
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today confirmed it is reviewing complaints linked to the evaluation process and upgrade works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil.
It said it is examining related information and documents before deciding on further investigation,
Berita Harian
reported this afternoon.
“MACC always takes seriously any allegations involving corruption, abuse of power and misconduct affecting public interest and government procurement,” it was quoted as saying.
It also urged those with information on corruption or irregularities to channel it directly to the agency instead of disclosing it publicly.
It said this was to prevent leaks and avoid disrupting investigations.
Local news outlets had previously reported allegations by contractors over a RM24 million stadium upgrading project.
The complaints were said to centre on two tenders tied to preparations for the 2027 SEA Games, involving a new running track and a protective flooring system used during concerts and major events.

Title: Video of Perlis MB shooting sacrificial cow sparks police probe
Article:
KANGAR, May 29 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah is set to be questioned by police after a video clip showed him shooting a cow with a rifle during an Aidiladha sacrifice ceremony yesterday.
The footage, which has since spread widely on social media, sparked criticism online, with some questioning the use of a firearm during the korban ritual and whether it complied with religious procedures.
Abu Bakar admitted to shooting the cow but said did it to prevent anyone from getting hurt after the animal had broken loose.
“I didn’t shoot the cow for fun. It had become aggressive. If we had left it alone and it gored someone to death, who would be held responsible?
“Once it broke loose, we couldn’t do anything else. So I took the rifle and shot it in the leg. After it fell over, then we slaughtered it,” he was quoted by news portal
Free Malaysia Today
as saying today.
